analysis.problem: Insufficient critical thinking and media literacy skills among the global population
analysis.root_cause: Lack of emphasis on critical thinking and media literacy in traditional education systems

proposed_actions.action: Integrate critical thinking and media literacy into school curricula
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the number of schools incorporating critical thinking and media literacy into their curricula by 20% within the next 2 years

evidence: Some researchers suggest that the study of literacy as a concept can be divided into two periods
In a world flooded with information, learning to think critically is more important than ever
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of biosphere and human dignity, as a lack of critical thinking and media literacy can lead to the spread of misinformation and undermine trust in institutions
analysis.summary: The global population lacks critical thinking and media literacy skills, which can be addressed by integrating these skills into school curricula. This problem has a medium severity level and requires attention from education policymakers and teachers.

raw_items.title: Global education
raw_items.summary: Global education is a mental development program that seeks to improve global human development based on the understanding of global dynamics, through the various sectors of human development delivery. raw_items.title: Literacy
raw_items.summary: Literacy is the ability to read and write, and illiteracy is the inability to read and write.
